raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
October 2024 closed with total actuals of $577,500 against a budget of $680,000, producing an unfavourable variance of -$102,500 (-15.1%). The shortfall is concentrated in Revenue, which missed plan by $82,000, and is substantially explained by three deal slippages into November and December driven by procurement delays and a champion change, compounded by one deal lost to a competitor on price. Professional Services and Cost of Revenue variances are directly downstream of the same pipeline slippage, as deferred onboardings and unexecuted SOWs reduced both billable activity and variable delivery costs in the month. The two remaining variances — Software Licenses and Support Contracts — are immaterial. Recovery of the slipped deals is expected in Q4, though Summit Retail Corp carries re-qualification risk and the timing of Axis Media Group's close remains subject to the engagement of their new procurement lead.

LINE COMMENTARY

Revenue | -$82,000 (-16.4%) | Budget: $500,000 | Actual: $418,000
The full $82,000 shortfall is accounted for by four pipeline outcomes in October. Three deals slipped into later months: Glenbrook Financial ($45,000 ARR, expected close 15 November, held on procurement signature), Axis Media Group ($28,000 ARR, expected close 1 December, procurement lead recently changed), and Summit Retail Corp ($9,000 ARR, expected close 30 November, undergoing re-qualification following a champion change). One deal — Clearwater Tech ($12,000 ARR) — was lost to competitor Apex AI on price and represents permanent revenue exposure with no recovery path. The three slipped deals are expected to absorb into November and December plan, though Summit's re-qualification status and the Axis procurement transition introduce timing uncertainty that requires confirmation before full recovery can be assumed.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-07T26:15:00Z - crm-revenue-broad - crm-oct-pipeline: October pipeline close analysis. 3 deals pushed from October to November/December: Glenbrook Financial (ARR $45,000, close moved to Nov 15 — procurement delay), Axis Media Group (ARR $28,000, close moved to Dec 1 — procurement delay), Summit Retail Corp (ARR $9,000, close moved to Nov 30 — champion changed, deal re-qualified). One deal lost: Clearwater Tech ($12,000 ARR) to competitor Apex AI on price. Total miss: $82,000.
- Slack - 2024-11-07T12:15:00Z - slack-revenue-broad - sales-slack-2024-10-31: October close recap — we missed plan by $82K. Three slips and one loss. Glenbrook procurement just needs a signature. Axis new procurement lead is engaged. Summit is a re-qualify risk. Clearwater went to Apex on price — nothing more we could do. November plan needs to absorb the three slips.

Professional Services | -$6,000 (-12.0%) | Budget: $50,000 | Actual: $44,000
The $6,000 shortfall is directly downstream of the Revenue pipeline slippage described above. Two SOW renewals — Glenbrook ($3,500) and Axis ($2,500) — are contractually ready to execute but cannot be invoiced until the respective primary software deals close. A third new PS engagement was deferred due to its onboarding dependency on the Summit Retail Corp close. The $6,000 is expected to be recoverable in line with the deal closures, though Summit's re-qualification risk may affect the timing of the third engagement.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-21T26:15:00Z - crm-professional_services-broad - crm-ps-oct: Two SOW renewals (Glenbrook $3,500, Axis $2,500) held pending primary deal close — will be booked when revenue deals sign. One new PS engagement delayed due to onboarding dependency on Summit close.
- Slack - 2024-11-21T12:15:00Z - slack-professional_services-broad - ps-slack-2024-10-31: PS miss is entirely downstream of software deals. Glenbrook and Axis PS SOWs are ready to execute but we can't invoice until the primary deals close. This will all normalise in November.

Cost of Revenue | -$12,000 (-15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $68,000
The $12,000 underspend reflects the variable nature of Cost of Revenue, which scales with customer onboardings and revenue recognition. The two enterprise onboardings for Glenbrook Financial and Axis Media Group — the largest components of planned October delivery activity — were pushed to November alongside their deals, reducing hosting, support, and delivery costs in the month. No Slack context was found for this line; the CRM record is the sole source, and the driver is consistent with the Revenue slippage pattern. The underspend is likely to reverse in November as those onboardings proceed, subject to deal close confirmation.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-15T26:15:00Z - crm-cost_of_revenue-broad - crm-cor-oct: $12,000 underspend reflects lower hosting and support costs from reduced new customer onboardings — two enterprise onboardings (Glenbrook, Axis) pushed to November with their deals. Variable COGS scales with revenue recognition.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are within acceptable tolerance and require no further commentary at this time.
- Software Licenses: -$3,000 (-10.0%)
- Support Contracts: +$500 (+2.5%)
